top_findings.finding: Participant could not locate the accordion section for health assessment information despite explicit task instructions and expressed confusion about how to find the required content.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant searched the page, noted only the account creation prompt, and stated inability to find the accordion expansion mechanism mentioned in the instructions.
top_findings.stated_signal: I can't really find it though. I don't see what you, what the, I don't get what the instructions say because they say to expand the thing, but like it's not there.
top_findings.rationale: The participant explicitly stated inability to locate the accordion section and expressed confusion about the mismatch between instructions (which reference an expandable accordion) and what is actually visible on the page. The observed behaviour shows the participant searching but not finding the required pathway, and the stated signal confirms frustration with the missing content. This blocks the component task of locating health assessment information.

top_findings.finding: Participant experienced garbled or corrupted text output from the screen reader while navigating the search interface and form elements.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant repeated nonsensical character sequences and fragmented words (H, Y G H A R E C K A L L F T T H A, Q I I, B N N S S T Y T R E R T Y U U T E C O, E R, R E E B N N C C D S R T R E E) while attempting to read page content, indicating severe accessibility output degradation.
top_findings.rationale: The transcript shows systematic, repeated corruption of screen reader output across multiple form and search elements. This is characteristic of significant accessibility friction affecting a blind or low-vision user relying on assistive technology. The participant recovered enough to proceed through the form, but the underlying barrier is substantial and unresolved.
top_findings.structural_amplification_note: Blind or screen reader-dependent user cohort. Screen reader output corruption or incompatible ARIA/semantic markup is a structural barrier that prevents reliable content access and significantly amplifies friction for this user group.

top_findings.finding: Participant experienced severe accessibility friction while attempting to use voice control and screen reader features, resulting in garbled text input and inability to execute a coherent search.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant attempted to use voice commands and assistive technology controls (mentioned 'rotor', 'speech off', keyboard navigation), which produced random character sequences ('Y G H A R E C K A L L F T T H A', 'B N N S S T Y T R E R T Y U U T E C') rather than forming a coherent search query.
top_findings.stated_signal: I would have written finance photography folder, voice memo apps, and Google folder opening Google Google. AI mode, Google search field. Text field, text field is editing.
top_findings.rationale: Both observed and stated signals show that assistive technology interaction produced unusable output. The participant's attempt to navigate Google search using voice and keyboard shortcuts resulted in corrupted input that did not form a valid search query. This is a significant accessibility barrier that blocked forward progress on the task.
top_findings.structural_amplification_note: Assistive technology user (screen reader and voice control reliance) encountered critical input method failure. The interaction model did not support reliable speech-to-text or keyboard-based form filling, resulting in garbled character sequences instead of meaningful search terms.

top_findings.finding: Participant experienced unmet expectations about the service delivery model when the website offered advisor-mediated quote instead of online self-service.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant proceeded through the website flow and reached a page stating an advisor would contact them, then immediately decided to try a different website.
top_findings.stated_signal: I thought they were going to give me like a quote online or something, but now they're saying that an advisor is going to get in touch with me, which actually I didn't think was going to happen. I didn't actually want that.
top_findings.rationale: The participant's stated expectation (online quote) conflicted with the actual interface outcome (advisor-mediated process). The participant explicitly rejected the outcome and abandoned the flow to try a competing website, indicating a significant barrier that bypassed the intended service model, matching S3 (significant component failure or barrier skipped).

top_findings.finding: Participant could not locate the health assessment information and accordion section mentioned in the task instructions despite searching the page.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant navigated through multiple page sections and stated inability to find the required content.
top_findings.stated_signal: I can't really find it though.
top_findings.rationale: The participant was tasked to locate health assessment information and expand an accordion section but explicitly stated inability to find it. This represents a component-level barrier where the required content pathway was not accessible to the participant, fitting the S3 gate (significant component failure).

coaching_recommendations.title: Address recording setup before deepening narration coaching
coaching_recommendations.summary: Both narration (sparse) and recording (poor) are below ideal. Improving the recording channel first will let later narration coaching land more reliably; isolated narration and recording recommendations are suppressed in favour of this combined item.
advice: Resolve recording-setup issues — microphone clarity, ambient noise, clipping — before pushing for richer narration.
Re-state think-aloud expectations to the participant only after the recording channel is reliable, so verbalisation is captured cleanly.
Treat current narration-quality readings as lower-confidence until recording stabilises.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-B narration=sparse ∧ recording=poor; isolated narration / recording recommendations are suppressed in favour of this meta item.

coaching_recommendations.title: Prioritise task-blocking friction before general coaching
coaching_recommendations.summary: The session contains 32 finding-level issue(s), including task-blocking or near-blocking severity (S2).
advice: Review the highest-severity findings before applying general coaching templates.
Prioritise issues that prevented or substantially disrupted task completion.
Use the highest-severity findings to guide focused follow-up coaching.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-A severity distribution: S2=1, S3=5, S4=8, S5=18. Representative findings: Participant could not locate the accordion section for health assessment information despite explicit task instructions and expressed confusion about how to find the required content. | Participant experienced garbled or corrupted text output from the screen reader while navigating the search interface and form elements. | Participant experienced severe accessibility friction while attempting to use voice control and screen reader features, resulting in garbled text input and inability to execute a coherent search.

coaching_recommendations.title: Multi-pattern friction across 7 categories — anchor coaching on the dominant pattern
coaching_recommendations.summary: This session shows 32 findings spanning 7 distinct friction types (F2,F1,F3,F6,F4,F5,F7). Coaching should follow the dominant pattern first rather than issuing one item per type.
advice: After addressing any severity-priority recommendation first, use this aggregation to group the remaining coaching themes — do not treat the two as parallel.
Treat F2 (Confidence) as the primary coaching anchor; secondary types (F1 Comprehension, F3 Accessibility) provide supporting context.
Avoid issuing parallel recommendations for each friction type when patterns interact within the same session.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-A: total=32 findings across 7 distinct friction types (F2=13, F1=4, F3=4, F6=4, F4=3, F5=3, F7=1).
